paper_gates.doctrine: STRATEGY_V1 paper gates: 4 green weeks + positive CLV = eligible to PRESENT for live arming - never auto-arm. Logged via the chat ritual; the app only displays.

plays.name: Play B - fear-window put credit spread
plays.status: armed on paper - awaiting trigger (VIX>=30 + quadrant A turn)
plays.note: sells the fear premium at the same No.3 moment; 30-45 DTE, short strike at the giveUp (~20-25 delta), credit >= 1/3 width, TP 50%, stop 2x, timeStop 21d, never through earnings

plays.name: Play C - post-earnings IV-crush call
plays.note: beat + RAISED guidance only, gap < +12%, no AVOID-tier; 45-60 DTE 0.4-0.5 delta after 10am; target +40-50%, giveUp gap-fill, timeStop 15td

plays.name: Play D - insider-cluster drift
plays.status: EDGAR scanner building the first watch list
plays.note: >=3 distinct insiders, >=$25k each, >=$250k total, officer-weighted; papers per Play C mechanics; 200d-interaction measured from day one

plays.name: Play E - earnings premium sale (defined-risk)
plays.status: cohort opens Jul 29-Aug 7 (twin of Play C, same calendar)
plays.note: SELL put credit spread / iron condor INTO the print, only where implied move >= 1.5x that name's own avg historical earnings move; defined-risk always, never naked; 15-20 tiny events per season (law of large numbers IS the sizing rule); buy back mechanically the morning after - the crush is captured in the first hour

plays.name: Play F - VIX-decay entry (specialty)
plays.status: awaiting a VIX>=30 spike, then the decay through 75% of peak
plays.note: enter as fear EXHALES, not at the spike: buy index/A-tier calls when VIX decays back through 75% of the episode peak; 60d horizon; Wave-1 long-history read 4.4% vs 2.3% baseline per event (sub-bar at N=36 - that is what the paper cohort is for)

plays.name: Play G - breadth capitulation (specialty)
plays.status: awaiting global breadth <30% onset (switchboard dial 3 collapse)
plays.note: when <30% of country ETFs hold their 200d (onset, not level), buy SPY/index structure, 60d horizon; OOS events averaged +5.9%/60d, small sample; rare window - pairs with the crisis ladder, fires earlier

research.doctrine: Distilled from the Jul 19-20 2026 research (all adversarially audited; failed claims removed). Each rule names WHAT the regime is, WHICH structure the data supports, and WHY. The app reads the live regime and surfaces the matching card - it never places an order.

regimes.label: DEEP FEAR
regimes.structure_index: SELL defined-risk put spreads (30-45 DTE, 25-delta short strike, width sized to 2% of account)
regimes.structure_names: BUY calls on high rip-rate names (45-60 DTE, 0.40-0.50 delta)
regimes.why: 30yr of real CBOE prices: every premium-selling structure beats long equity ONLY when VIX>25 (^PUT +22.5%/yr excess, ^BXM +17.2%, JEPI +27.3%). Modelled at the fear trigger, long ATM calls returned -9.5% mean while 25-delta put spreads returned +10.8%. Names carry a 15.0% chance of a >25% rip vs 0.8% for indexes - that right tail is what a long call is paying for.
regimes.management: put spreads: take 50% of max profit (raises win rate 91%->94%), hard stop at 2x credit (cuts worst case -100%->-42%). Calls: target +40-50%, giveUp = gap-fill, timeStop 15td.
regimes.never: naked puts (margin expands while you lose), premium selling on single names (6.9% crash rate vs 3.2% index), 0DTE (retail loses 4.7% relative - the bid-ask eats the premium)

regimes.label: FEAR BAND
regimes.structure_index: Put credit spreads armed - the wider band carries ~2.5x the sample of the >=30 trigger
regimes.structure_names: Watch only; the call edge concentrates at deeper fear
regimes.why: The VIX 24-35 band gave 18 historical episodes vs 9 for VIX>=30. Premium selling turns profitable vs long equity somewhere around VIX 25 across all 14 real-price strategies tested.
regimes.management: identical: 50% profit target, 2x credit stop, never through earnings
regimes.never: sizing up because the win rate looks high - one loss costs ~10 wins

regimes.label: STRENGTH
regimes.structure_index: Long exposure. ENTRY TIMING DOES NOT MATTER - buy now.
regimes.structure_names: Long exposure or long calls on rip-rate names; same finding on timing
regimes.why: Entry tournament, n=3052 decisions: every timing rule (dip 2/3/5%, 20d/50d MA touch, breakout, thrust) landed within +/-0.35% of simply buying immediately on a +6.23% baseline. Breakout-buying was NEGATIVE (-0.11%). The edge is regime participation, not entry precision.
regimes.never: systematic covered calls / income selling here - QYLD lost 11.1%/yr to QQQ over 12 years; every buy-write structure bleeds in calm tapes

regimes.label: CALM DRIFT (retired)
regimes.structure_index: Nothing. The old No.1 calm-drift play is a well-sampled null.
regimes.why: n=88 episodes: +0.24% excess over baseline, p=0.617. The VIX<20 gate itself contributed -1.61% (p~0.046) - waiting for calm excluded the profitable part of the distribution.
regimes.never: treating this as an edge - it was doctrine, not evidence

vehicle_rip_rates.note: P(a >25% move up within 63td of a dip+trend signal). THE metric for call-vehicle selection - mean return is the wrong one. From 2,900 signals across the app universe.

killed.why: premium is real but retail loses 4.7% relative - the bid-ask on cheap options eats it; 252 decisions/yr is the churn era institutionalised
TAIL cost 22.3%/yr vs SPY over 9 years; crisis-ladder cash does the same job free

killed.what: systematic covered calls / PMCC income sleeve
killed.why: QYLD -11.1%/yr vs QQQ; every buy-write underperformed. Re-scope the $25k milestone to CONDITIONAL premium selling at VIX>25
6.9% of name signals fell >20% in 63td vs 3.2% for indexes - and that understates it, since dead tickers are missing from the data
-0.11% vs buying now, the only timing rule that was negative

last.role: EVP, Man., Logistics and Op Ex

book.note: EXHIBIT - an illustrative example book, not a real account. The private build syncs the real one from the broker and reprices it live.

positions.plan: EXAMPLE - index swing: first tranche <=1/3, add one at -5% deeper, hard exit below the give-up. Exits declared at entry, in a cold moment.
EXAMPLE - the fear-dip edge expressed deep-ITM (0.76 delta): minimal extrinsic, so the IV crush can't eat a correct call. ~63 trading-day hold.
EXAMPLE - strength-mode ride on an A-tier leader. Sell into strength; hard-off if it loses the 200-day.
EXAMPLE - CORE ballast bought in a fear window. Never sold; it exists so the desk keeps firing through drawdowns.

breaches: QQQ = 18% of equity in one print (engraved cap 6%)
SPY = 12% of equity in one print (engraved cap 6%)
NVDA = 11% of equity in one print (engraved cap 6%)

longgame.doctrine: GARGANTUA is an OPTIONS app — the desk is the engine, always. The core sleeve is BALLAST: bought only at validated fear/crisis spots with a slice of harvested wins, so the desk keeps firing through drawdowns. It never leads, never buys on a schedule, and never competes with the desk for powder.

milestones.name: ballast seeding unlocks
milestones.note: a slice of harvested wins into shares at fear/crisis windows

milestones.name: ballast share grows
milestones.note: still bought only at the spots — never on a schedule

next_milestone.name: ballast share grows
next_milestone.note: still bought only at the spots — never on a schedule
